sanskrit:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान् गुणान्।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु॥
transliteration:
puruṣaḥ prakṛti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛti-jān guṇān
kāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu
literal_meaning: The conscious person, situated in material nature, experiences the qualities born of nature; the cause is his attachment to those qualities—hence births in higher and lower wombs.
contextual_meaning: We are not punished by the world; we are enrolled in the curriculum we keep choosing. Every mood (guṇa) we cling to writes the syllabus for our next classroom—whether that classroom is a palace, a cubicle, or a hospital bed.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: realizing that the felt sense of being a victim co-exists with an implicit freedom to change mental company kept.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for dopamine hits, noticing the algorithm keeps feeding darker content—realizing the feed is mirroring the emotional ‘guṇa’ you keep engaging with.
anchor_text: Your attachments design your next classroom.
